prompt: Create 1000 meter service-area buffers around all hospital points in Los Angeles County and clip the result to the study boundary. Save a GeoPackage named T001_buffer_hospitals.gpkg.
Create 2500 meter hospital service-area buffers, use a metric CRS for distance, clip to the county boundary, and save T002_buffer_hospitals_2500m.gpkg.
Create a dissolved 1 km hospital coverage layer for Los Angeles County. Use safe metric buffering and save T003_buffer_hospitals_dissolved.gpkg.
Buffer hospitals by 1000 meters, preserve hospital name and source_id fields after clipping, and save T004_buffer_hospitals_named.gpkg.
Create 1 km hospital buffers, clip to Los Angeles County, compute area_m2 for each clipped buffer in a projected CRS, and save T005_buffer_hospitals_area.gpkg.
Intersect Los Angeles County census tracts with flood zones and save the polygon overlay as T006_flood_tract_overlay.gpkg.
Create a flood exposure overlay by intersecting census tracts and flood zones. Compute flood_area_m2 in a metric CRS and save T007_flood_tract_overlay_area.gpkg.
Intersect census tracts with flood zones and preserve the tract_id and sfha_tf fields. Save T008_sfha_overlay.gpkg.
Estimate flood-zone area per census tract. Intersect tracts and flood zones, aggregate flood area by tract_id, join it back to tracts, and save T009_flood_summary_by_tract.gpkg.
Create a tract layer containing flood_area_m2 and keep only tracts with any flood exposure. Save T010_high_flood_tracts.gpkg.
Count public schools inside each census tract and save the tract polygons with school_count as T011_school_count_by_tract.gpkg.
Count hospitals inside each census tract and save the tract polygons with hospital_count as T012_hospital_count_by_tract.gpkg.
Count schools per census tract, compute schools_per_km2 using land area, and save T013_school_density_by_tract.gpkg.
Create a census tract layer with school_count and keep only tracts with at least one school. Save T014_tracts_with_schools.gpkg.
For each census tract, compute both school_count and hospital_count, and save T015_school_hospital_counts.gpkg.
Clip the temperature raster to the Los Angeles County study boundary and save T016_temperature_clip.tif.
Clip the temperature raster to the county boundary, preserve nodata handling, and save T017_temperature_clip_nodata.tif.
Clip temperature_raster.tif using study_boundary.gpkg. Reproject the boundary to the raster CRS before masking and save T018_temperature_clip_boundary_crs.tif.
Clip the temperature raster to Los Angeles County and save a compressed GeoTIFF named T019_temperature_clip_compressed.tif.
Clip the temperature raster to the study boundary while preserving CRS, transform, nodata, and metadata. Save T020_temperature_clip_metadata.tif.
Compute mean and sum temperature for each census tract using temperature_raster.tif and save T021_temperature_zonal_stats.gpkg.
Compute mean temperature per census tract, handle CRS alignment, and save T022_temperature_mean_by_tract.gpkg.
Compute temperature zonal statistics for tracts and keep the hottest quartile by temp_mean. Save T023_temperature_stats_hot_tracts.gpkg.
Compute mean temperature and school_count for each tract, then save a combined tract layer named T024_temperature_school_join.gpkg.
Create a tract-level layer with temp_mean, school_count, and flood_area_m2. Save T025_temperature_flood_school.gpkg.
Create a choropleth PNG of mean temperature by census tract using a sequential blue color scheme, title, legend, and source note. Save T026_temperature_choropleth.png.
Create a choropleth PNG of school density by census tract with a clear legend and title. Save T027_school_density_choropleth.png.
Create a choropleth PNG of flood exposure area by census tract with an appropriate sequential color ramp. Save T028_flood_exposure_choropleth.png.
Create a PNG map showing hospital count per census tract, with title, legend, readable borders, and source note. Save T029_hospital_access_choropleth.png.
Create a tract-level PNG map that communicates combined heat, flood exposure, and school availability risk. Explain choices in code comments and save T030_multivariate_risk_map.png.
